content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 8, 1994

Karl Ketner
Database Management
& Custom Programming
1051 Broadway
San Francisco, CA 94133

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-93-464

Dear Mr. Ketner:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regarding the applicability of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ to a San Francisco Campaign Finance Ordinance, Proposition X, that was passed by the voters on November 2, 1993. Because your question is general in nature and you have not indicated that you are seeking advice on behalf of a specific person with duties under the Act, we are treating your letter as a request for informal assistance.²

The Act requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b)).

The Commission is precluded from providing advice to third parties under certain circumstances. (2 Cal. Code Regulations Section 18329(b)(8).) For example, the Commission does not provide advice to third parties regarding another person's duties under the Act unless he or she has been authorized to do so by that person. Nor does the Commission render advice regarding past conduct.

Consequently, while we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the primary issue you raise in your letter, namely, the validity of Proposition X, we have enclosed some general materials for your information. These include a copy of Commission

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. G-93-464
Page 2


Regulation 18421.1 which discusses, in part, the receipt of
contributions, and Government Code Sections 81009.5 and 81013.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.3

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature: Deanne Stone]

                                        By:  Deanne Stone
                                        Senior Commission Counsel
SGC:DRS
Enclosures




3     Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: Whether the Political Reform Act applies to a San Francisco Campaign Finance Ordinance, Proposition X, that was passed by the voters on November 2, 1993.
sections.conclusion: The Commission is unable to provide assistance with respect to the primary issue raised, namely, the validity of Proposition X, because the question is general in nature and the requestor has not indicated they are seeking advice on behalf of a specific person with duties under the Act. Informal assistance is being provided instead, with enclosed general materials regarding contribution receipt and related regulations.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether the Political Reform Act applies to San Francisco's Proposition X, a campaign finance ordinance passed by voters in November 1993.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide formal advice on the validity of Proposition X because the question was general in nature and not on behalf of a specific person with duties under the Act, but provided general informational materials instead.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an informal advice response letter (not formal written advice) classified as correspondence. The document lacks traditional 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear question and conclusion content in letter format. File number appears as both '1-93-464' and 'G-93-464' in the document.

QUESTION: Whether the Political Reform Act applies to a San Francisco Campaign Finance Ordinance, Proposition X, that was passed by the voters on November 2, 1993.

CONCLUSION: The Commission is unable to provide assistance with respect to the primary issue raised, namely, the validity of Proposition X, because the question is general in nature and the requestor has not indicated they are seeking advice on behalf of a specific person with duties under the Act. Informal assistance is being provided instead, with enclosed general materials regarding contribution receipt and related regulations.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to Karl Ketner's request for informal assistance regarding the applicability of the Political Reform Act to San Francisco's Proposition X campaign finance ordinance, declining to provide formal advice on the primary issue but enclosing general materials on contributions and related regulations.
